sources.use: DSSE signature verification and key identifier semantics
Supply-chain authorization model in which cryptographic keys represent functionaries

finding: A threshold over distinct keyid strings is not a threshold over distinct cryptographic signers. The same public key can be registered under multiple aliases and satisfy the former unless verified keys are deduplicated by canonical public-key material.
design_implication: Compute SHA-256 over canonical DER SubjectPublicKeyInfo for every successfully verified key and apply the signature threshold to distinct fingerprints, while retaining key IDs only as diagnostic labels.

checks: one valid Ed25519 signer passes threshold 1
two cryptographically distinct Ed25519 signers pass threshold 2
one Ed25519 key exposed under two key IDs fails threshold 2
verification.repository_ci: not observed for the exact commits during this run

evidence_strength: committed source plus deterministic isolated runtime
uncertainty: The verifier still relies on caller-supplied trust anchors and does not establish signer authorization, certificate-chain validity, transparency-log inclusion, trusted time, or claim truth.
falsification_route: Construct two different SPKI encodings that resolve to the same effective verification key but yield different canonical DER fingerprints, or demonstrate a supported key type for which canonical SPKI export is unstable across equivalent keys.
privacy_boundary: Public standards, public repository source, and synthetic generated keys only; no private user material.
